channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Una historia de amor turca, con cárcel, poesía y una hazaña aérea
item.description: Tras una confesión forzada y una condena a muerte, un poeta kurdo pasó 30 años en la cárcel, donde descubrió su voz. Su promotora literaria, para sorpresa de todos, se convirtió en su compañera de vida.
item.dc:creator: Silvana Paternostro

media:content.media:credit: Bradley Secker para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El poeta Ilhan Sami Çomak, a la derecha, y su pareja, Ipek Ozel, a la izquierda, en su casa de Estambul.

item.title: La solución infalible que Trump busca para poner fin a la guerra de Irán tal vez no exista
item.description: El presidente de EE. UU. intenta aumentar la presión económica sobre Teherán, pero es poco probable que el gobierno iraní acepte negociar sin un acuerdo significativo y digno.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una valla publicitaria en Teherán la semana pasada, que muestra un estrecho de Ormuz cerrado sobre la boca del presidente Trump.

item.title: The Met Gala
item.description: What were your favorite and least favorite looks at this year’s event?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Pollution Worsened in South Bronx After Congestion Tolls, Study Finds
item.description: Columbia University researchers said that sensors detected a 2 percent increase in toxic particles in the air. M.T.A. officials disputed the report.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard and Olivia Bensimon

media:content.media:credit: Jade Doskow for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The South Bronx, which is divided by major highways and bridges, has some of the highest asthma rates in the United States.

item.title: A Writer With a Healthy Appetite, and a Love of New York City
item.description: To cover the city’s evolving food scene, Luke Fortney relies on his curiosity, and an assortment of fermented dishes.
item.dc:creator: Dori Gray

media:content.media:credit: Heather Willensky for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Luke Fortney is a writer of the Where to Eat: New York City newsletter, where he offers readers restaurant recommendations.

item.title: Trial Begins for Man Accused of Running Secret Police Outpost for China
item.description: Lu Jianwang, a U.S. citizen, is accused of working on behalf of the Chinese government to target dissidents. A co-defendant has pleaded guilty.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.category: Ministry of Public Security of the People's Republic of China

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Seng for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lu Jianwang is accused of working on behalf of China out of an outpost in Manhattan.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 6, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the California Gubernatorial Debate
item.description: Seven candidates sparred in the first nationally televised debate in the 2026 California governor’s race. Many tried to launch attacks in a last-ditch effort to gain ground on their opponents.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jill Cowan

media:content.media:description: Seven candidates engaged in a spirited debate hosted by CNN at East Los Angeles College on Tuesday.

item.title: Late Night Cringes at Trump’s Chatter in Front of Children
item.description: “How are you talking about nuclear war in front of children?” Desi Lydic asked after the president discussed the Iran conflict in a room full of students on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: China Meets Iranian Official as U.S. Demands Strait of Hormuz Open
item.description: China’s top diplomat was meeting with Iran’s foreign minister, the first senior Iranian official to visit Beijing since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Seyed Abbas Araqchi, via Telegram, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing, China, on Wednesday.

item.title: Qué significa ‘attest’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 105 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: attest
item.description: This word has appeared in 105 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: La Casa Blanca plantea revisar los modelos de IA antes de su estreno
item.description: El gobierno de EE. UU. debate una orden ejecutiva para crear un grupo de trabajo que reúna a ejecutivos tecnológicos y funcionarios del gobierno para examinar procedimientos de supervisión.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Dustin Volz
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El cambio de política del gobierno de Trump comenzó después de que Anthropic presentara un potente modelo de inteligencia artificial llamado Mythos.

item.title: Cómo la IA está transformando la industria china del entretenimiento
item.description: El apogeo de los dramas generados por IA ha provocado protestas. Los actores afirman que les quita oportunidades y algunos videos usan imágenes de celebridades sin autorización.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: My Father Wrote Letters to the Cuban Government. Here Is Mine.
item.description: Please do not take the fact that I am writing this letter to you as a defense of U.S. policy toward Cuba, much less a call for American intervention.
item.dc:creator: Ada Ferrer

item.title: French Literature Is in an Uproar
item.description: Changes at a famous French publishing house tell us a lot about the world we live in now.
item.dc:creator: Olivier Guez

item.title: Oil Falls and Stocks Gain as Trump Changes Course in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices fell and stocks rose after President Trump’s about-face to pause the U.S. operation to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Illinois State Police to Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting
item.description: Officials said they were examining the shooting of Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez last summer during a Chicago-area crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:description: A memorial for Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez, who was shot by an ICE agent in Franklin Park, Ill., last year.

item.title: El gobierno de Trump busca expulsar a los bisontes de Montana
item.description: El gobierno de Trump está desalojando a manadas de bisontes de las praderas federales, después de ponerse del lado de los ganaderos y los líderes republicanos enfrentados a los ecologistas y los líderes tribales.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy and John Stember

media:content.media:description: American Prairie has identified the northern grasslands of Montana as the only place left in the United States to restore an entire prairie ecosystem.

item.title: Guy Goma’s Accidental BBC Interview Lives On After 20 Years
item.description: Guy Goma thought he was interviewing for an I.T. job when he found himself on air on the BBC. What came next was familiar to anyone who has been unprepared at work.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Pitches Strength and Stability in Visits to Australia and Vietnam
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is attempting to raise Japan’s profile at a time when China’s clout is rising and doubts about the United States are spreading.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan arriving in Canberra, Australia, on Sunday.

item.title: Corrections: May 6, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, May 6, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Taiwan Learns From Ukraine as Informal Connections Grow
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, May 6, 2026.

item.title: Democrats Keep Michigan Senate Majority With Special Election Win
item.description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a Democrat, allowed the swing district seat to sit vacant for more than a year as her party clung to a slim majority.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Sam Easter

item.title: Suspect in Colorado Firebombing Attack Will Plead Guilty, Court Records Say
item.description: Mohamed Sabry Soliman, who was charged in an attack that injured more than a dozen people and killed one, will be sentenced to life in prison, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Mohamed Sabry Soliman, at right, during the attack last year.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: May 6, 2026
item.description: James Mattina’s debut blooms.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Katherine Marks for The New York Times

item.title: Takeaways From Indiana Primary Elections 2026: Trump Gets Payback
item.description: The president helped unseat most of the state lawmakers he targeted after they rebuffed his call to draw new House maps to help Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Indiana Statehouse in Indianapolis.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Eastern Pacific, Killing 3
item.description: The attack was the second in two days, and the latest in an ongoing campaign against people the United States says are engaged in drug smuggling at sea.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: U.S. Southern Command, led by Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, announced the attack on Tuesday evening.

item.title: Ex-ICE Deputy Falls Short in Republican House Primary in Ohio
item.description: Madison Sheahan, whose candidacy promised to test how general-election voters viewed President Trump’s immigration agenda, lost to a rival who billed himself as a “MAGA Republican.”
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call, via Associated Press; Leah Millis/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Madison D. Sheahan, right, stepped down from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to run for the House in Ohio, but she was defeated in the Republican primary race by Derek Merrin, left, a former state legislator.

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and Pro-Israel Demonstrators Rally Outside Synagogue
item.description: For the second time in six months, protesters gathered outside Park East Synagogue, which was holding an event to promote real estate sales in Israel. A heavy police presence kept the two groups apart.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Protesters opposed to the event at Park East Synagogue included pro-Palestinian groups and members of an Orthodox sect.

item.title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
item.description: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Despite Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s declaration that the objectives of the war have been accomplished, they clearly have not.

item.title: A Solitary Protester on Top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in D.C.
item.description: For five days, a 45-year-old former jeweler has camped on top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge, drawing a mix of support, disapproval and concern.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel and JoAnna Daemmrich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Guido Reichstadter atop the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in Washington, D.C. on Saturday. His protest there has gone on longer than he had planned.

item.title: Trump Posts Meme of Top Democrats in Sombreros
item.description: The fake image of Representative Hakeem Jeffries of New York and Senator Chuck Schumer of New York was similar to a post from September that was widely denounced as racist.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:description: Hakeem Jeffries, the House minority leader, and Chuck Schumer, the Senate minority leader, outside the White House after meeting with President Trump in September.

item.title: In Indiana Primary Elections, Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Incumbents
item.description: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:description: Voters at the polls in West Lafayette, Ind., on Tuesday.

item.title: ‘Avatar’ Suit Focuses on Hot Topic in A.I. Age: A Character’s Face
item.description: An actress accused the director James Cameron of stealing her likeness for a digitally created, blue-skinned warrior princess.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Vance Campaigns in Iowa as G.O.P. Fears Rise Ahead of Midterms
item.description: The vice president acknowledged economic headwinds, including rising energy and fertilizer costs. “We got a little — a little blip in the Middle East,” he said, referring to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking during a visit to Ex-Guard Industries on Tuesday in Des Moines.

item.title: Venezuela tiene el camino libre para empezar a reestructurar su deuda
item.description: El país debe devolver 60.000 millones de dólares en bonos impagados mientras intenta estabilizar su economía.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

media:content.media:description: Calles de la capital de Venezuela, Caracas, la semana pasada. El gobierno de Trump dijo que ha empezado a enviar millones de dólares de ingresos petroleros al gobierno de Venezuela.

item.title: Ohio Governor’s Race Set Between Vivek Ramaswamy and Amy Acton
item.description: In a close race, Vivek Ramaswamy, the Republican nominee, and Dr. Amy Acton, the Democrat, bring distinctive potential liabilities: his wealth and her time leading Ohio’s pandemic response.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

item.title: Journalist and Her Mother Are Among 3 Killed in Upper Manhattan Fire
item.description: Yolaine Díaz, a fashion and beauty writer for People en Español, and her mother were killed in the blaze at an apartment building in Inwood, People magazine said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Yolaine Diaz, Ana Mirtha Lantigua and an unidentified third person were pronounced dead at the scene of a fire in the Inwood neighborhood of Manhattan on Monday.

item.title: How Ancient Centipede Ancestors Conquered the Earth
item.description: A long-neglected fossil seems to show the evolutionary leap that let the ancestors of today’s many-legged arthropods crawl forth from the seas.
item.dc:creator: Jack Tamisiea
item.category: Proceedings of the Royal Society B (Journal)

item.title: The Historical Roots of Postliberalism
item.description: Postliberalism may seem like a contemporary movement, with champions like Vice President JD Vance and Patrick Deneen. But the tradition of liberal skepticism reaches much further back. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the historian Helena Rosenblatt looks back to 19th-century England to explain.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: What Is Liberal in the Liberal Arts?
item.description: What was a liberal arts education designed to offer? More than recent discourse might lead you to believe. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the historian Helena Rosenblatt explains why the liberal arts have strayed so far from their roots as a training ground for civic virtues and citizenry.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Colorado Snowstorm In May Could Be Its Biggest This Season
item.description: A snowstorm hitting the Rocky Mountains this week could end up being the biggest all season, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:description: Snow fell in Boulder, Colo., on Tuesday.

item.title: Russia Launches Deadly Attack in Ukraine Ahead of Cease-Fire
item.description: Russian forces launched an attack on three Ukrainian cities on Tuesday, killing more than 20 people and injuring dozens of others. The attack came after Russia announced a cease-fire timed to Moscow’s Victory Day celebrations on May 9, commemorating the Soviet victory over the Nazis in World War II.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:description: The site of a Russian airstrike in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, on Tuesday. The death toll in Zaporizhzhia was one of the highest from a single attack so far this year.

item.title: Un raro cometa aparece en los cielos del hemisferio sur
item.description: Conocido como C/2025 R3 PANSTARRS, el cometa será visible para los observadores de estrellas que utilicen un telescopio en Nueva Zelanda, Australia y Sudáfrica.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: U.S. Allows Venezuela to Begin Debt Restructuring Process
item.description: Venezuela must repay $60 billion in defaulted bonds as it looks to stabilize its economy.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

media:content.media:description: Streets in Venezuela’s capital, Caracas, last week. The Trump administration has said that it has started sending millions of dollars of oil revenue to Venezuela’s government.

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
item.description: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: A cruise ship remained stranded off the coast of Cape Verde after a suspected hantavirus outbreak on the vessel killed at least three people and left several others ill.

item.title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rip Into Ukrainian Cities
item.description: At least 12 people were killed in Zaporizhzhia, one of the highest tolls from a single attack so far this year. The cities of Dnipro and Kramatorsk were also attacked.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:description: The site of a Russian airstrike in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, on Tuesday. The death toll in Zaporizhzhia was one of the highest from a single attack so far this year.

item.title: Indiana’s Primary Elections Will Test Trump’s Influence
item.description: Also, about 150 people are stuck on a cruise ship with a deadly outbreak. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Voting in West Lafayette, Ind. today.

item.title: Elon Musk Wanted OpenAI to Go Commercial, Greg Brockman Testifies
item.description: Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president, testified in a trial pitting Mr. Musk against his company that the world’s richest man was eager to change how it operated as a nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: OpenAI’s president, Greg Brockman, with his wife, Anna Brockman, at the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif., on Tuesday. He testified for a second day.
